mnemonics: P.A.T.H. - Path-connected And Totally Helpful. Remember that a connected metric space is one that cannot be split into two disjoint non-empty open sets, like a single continuous path.
keyPoints: A metric space is connected if it is not the union of two disjoint non-empty open sets.
Continuous functions preserve connectedness; the image of a connected set under a continuous function is connected.
Path-connectedness implies connectedness, but the converse is not always true in general metric spaces.
commonMistakes: Assuming all connected sets are path-connected; while path-connected sets are connected, the reverse is not always true (e.g., the topologist's sine curve).
Confusing 'disconnected' with 'not open'; a set is disconnected if it can be partitioned into two non-empty, disjoint open sets relative to the subspace topology.
